data.title: Dow Jones futures impacted by Trump's 100% tariff threat on Canada, pending government shutdown, and looming earnings from Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, Apple.
data.description: President Donald Trump has warned of imposing a complete tariff of 100% on Canada. There is a heightened risk of a government shutdown. Big tech giants such as Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, and Apple face uncertainties in their financial outcomes. The Dow Jones Futures are anticipated to be influenced by the potential Trump tariffs, concerns over a government shutdown, and the upcoming earnings reports from major companies.

data.title: Zoomex enhances derivatives options for European traders, prioritizing stability and clear rules, meeting demands in 2026.
data.description: In Victoria, Seychelles, as of January 25, 2026, European traders are increasingly demanding specific requirements from trading platforms. These include comprehensive derivatives coverage, reliable execution in volatile markets, and transparent guidelines concerning access and withdrawals, rather than just a wide array of available cryptocurrencies. Zoomex is notably one of the platforms that frequently meet these criteria as preferred by traders.

data.title: BTC price drops before weekly close, challenges $86K mark amid potential volatility triggers at US market open.
data.description: As Bitcoin saw a decline in its gains, the trend coincided with the impending opening of US futures markets. This development comes as markets brace themselves for a series of potential catalysts that could lead to increased volatility and downward pressure.

data.title: Author Robert Kiyosaki unfazed by Bitcoin and Ethereum price fluctuations, predicts Bitcoin reaching $1 million.
data.description: Renowned author and investment expert Robert Kiyosaki remains unfazed by the fluctuating prices of Bitcoin and Ethereum. Despite the volatility, he continues to advocate for investing in both cryptocurrencies. Kiyosaki's recent interest in Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency globally, reflects his belief in the potential of these digital assets. He predicts that Bitcoin could soar to $1 million in value within the next few years or decade. Kiyosaki emphasizes that he bases his investment decisions on factors like the increasing US national debt and the declining purchasing power of the US dollar. His views on Bitcoin and Ethereum prices are shared in a recent post on X platform. For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bill Miller predicts Bitcoin could reach $1.7 million if recognized as 'digital gold', citing market cap parity calculation.
data.description: According to the Chairman and CIO of Miller Value Partners, Bill Miller IV, he believes that Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) has the potential to reach an outstanding value of $1.7 million per coin if it establishes itself as the equivalent of 'digital gold'. This assertion by Miller has drawn attention as gold prices surge to record highs, sparking debates on the perceived connection between the traditional precious metal and the leading cryptocurrency. Miller maintains that the lack of correlation between the two assets is significant. The envisaged price projection for Bitcoin, as suggested by Miller, is based on a market capitalization parity calculation. He explains that for Bitcoin to attain the entire monetary premium currently held by gold, its value would need to increase by a factor of approximately 19 from its present rate. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: DTC SEO Agency expands Ecommerce Search with AI, AEO, and GEO Attribution on Jan 23, 2026.
data.description: DTC SEO Agency has broadened its ecommerce search services to include AI Search, AEO, and GEO Attribution. This expansion was announced on 23-Jan-2026 by the agency through PR Newswire. The company clarified that the initial reference to "DTC SEO Agency" in the release should have been hyperlinked.
US House China Panel's Moolenaar: We can't let China weaponize an app to weaken the US.

data.title: MN's top companies urge for 'de-escalation' amid recent tensions, avoiding direct criticism of recent killings by immigration agents. #Minnesota #CorporateLeadership
data.description: Some prominent companies in the state, including Target and Cargill, recently issued a letter from their chief executives that falls just short of outright condemning the recent killings by federal immigration agents. The letter highlights the concern within the business community regarding this issue.

data.title: SharpLink Gaming plans to become the most 'focused, disciplined' Ethereum treasury by 2026, prioritizing long-term value for shareholders.
data.description: SharpLink Gaming, a Ethereum treasury company, aims to differentiate itself from competitors by prioritizing long-term goals and keeping shareholders as the primary focus.

data.title: Tezos' 20th upgrade in Tallinn, cuts block times to 6 secs without a fork; live implementation active.
data.description: Tezos recently celebrated its 20th major update since its inception in 2018 with the Tallinn protocol upgrade. This milestone was achieved without the need for a network fork, showcasing the platform's ability to evolve seamlessly. The continuous development and improvement process has been instrumental in Tezos' growth, with each update enhancing the platform's functionality and security.

data.title: ADP reports job loss due to govt shutdown, prompting Fed rate cuts; pending official jobs report delay.
data.description: The significance of the ADP jobs report has heightened due to the probable delay in the official jobs report caused by the government shutdown. ADP has reported an unexpected decline in jobs, prompting discussions about potential Federal Reserve rate cuts.

data.title: ADP's report reveals a decline in private payrolls, impacting S&P 500. BLS official report pending.
data.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) will release the official jobs report after the Federal Reserve meeting. The recent ADP Jobs Report revealed an unexpected decrease in private payrolls. This led to fluctuations in the S&P 500 index. The exact numbers from the ADP report and the impact on the S&P 500 were not disclosed.

data.title: Fed sees job openings rise, forecasts only one rate cut in 2026, easing hawkish fears. Context key.
data.description: The latest Federal Reserve projections indicate a potential single interest rate cut in 2026, suggesting a more restrained approach to monetary policy. Context is important, notably in light of the significant increase in job openings post-Fed meeting. While some may be concerned about a potential hawkish shift, the overall outlook remains positive.

data.title: US stock futures plunged as Trump threatened escalating tariffs on Europe over the Greenland dispute. Dow Jones -1.66%, S&P 500 -1.79%, Nasdaq 100 -2.23%, Russell 2000 -2.17%. Important numbers: 10-year Treasury bond at 4.28%, 2-year bond at 3.57%. SPY down 1.40% to $682.01, QQQ down 1.67% to $610.68. BHP down 1.65% despite production guidance lift. Alibaba dropped 2.35%. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged in January.
data.description: U.S. stock futures declined after a negative holiday break, influenced by President Trump's threat of escalating tariffs on Europe over the Greenland dispute. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.28% and the two-year bond was at 3.57%. Markets projected a 95% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in January. Index Performance: Dow Jones -1.66%, S&P 500 -1.79%, Nasdaq 100 -2.23%, Russell 2000 -2.17%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF were lower premarket. BHP Group fell 1.65% despite lifting copper production guidance. Alibaba Group Holding dropped 2.35% amidst competition concerns with ByteDance. The SPY was down 1.40% at $682.01, and the QQQ declined 1.67% to $610.68.

data.title: Fed inflation rate below forecast, rate cut likely. S&P 500 rallies post-key data.
data.description: The current inflation rate is falling below the Federal Reserve's prediction, indicating a possible interest rate cut in the future. Following this news, the S&P 500 experienced a rally.

data.title: Consumer confidence is down, employment has fallen, with ADP reporting job losses through early November, supporting a potential Fed rate cut.
data.description: As per ADP's latest weekly jobs report, the economy experienced a decline in the number of jobs until early November. Concurrently, the consumer confidence index dropped significantly and employment rates also fell during this period. These trends are speculated to potentially lead to an adjustment in Federal Reserve policies, with the possibility of a rate cut being considered by the Fed.

data.title: Dow futures drop, Nasdaq rises, tech stocks rebound. Jobless claims fall to 198k. Inflation higher, Treasury bond yields at 4.15%. Dow +0.11%, S&P 500 +0.36%, Nasdaq 100 +0.75%. Goldman Sachs reports earnings above expectations.
data.description: U.S. stock futures fluctuated following a lower close, with major benchmark indices showing mixed performance. Initial jobless claims unexpectedly dropped to 198,000, highlighting labor market tightness, while manufacturing activity in the Northeast rebounded. U.S. import prices rose 0.4% in November. The Nasdaq Composite fell 1% on news of a 25% tariff on select semiconductor imports. The Supreme Court delayed a ruling on tariff legality. 10-year Treasury yielded 4.15%, two-year bond at 3.52%. Market projects Fed to keep interest rates steady in January. Stock futures climbed premarket, with SPY up 0.40% at $693.13 and QQQ up 0.91% at $625.16. Goldman Sachs reported quarterly earnings of $14.01 per share, beating estimates, causing its stock to decline by 1.79% in premarket trading. Goldman Sachs maintains a strong price trend over the short, medium, and long term, with solid quality ranking.

data.title: Jobless rate almost 4-year high before Fed meeting on Dec. 10, impacting rate-cut chances. Hiring improves.
data.description: The upcoming jobs report, just before the Federal Reserve meeting on December 10, holds significant weight in determining the possibility of a rate cut. The latest data indicates the jobless rate has reached a nearly 4-year high, yet there has been an increase in hiring activity. This development could sway the decision-making process regarding interest rate adjustments.

data.title: ADP Jobs report beats expectations, impacting Fed outlook; S&P 500 stable. Ripple for December rate cut uncertain.
data.description: There is uncertainty regarding a December rate cut as the latest ADP Jobs Report surpassed expectations, creating confusion for the Fed's decision. The S&P 500 remained stable amidst these developments.

data.title: China halted rare earth exports before trade truce; stocks drop.#imports and exports of rare earths, impact on markets.
data.description: Before the recent escalation in tensions, China had already refused to export rare earth materials to defense companies. Following the trade truce between Trump and Xi, questions remain as rare earth stocks experienced a decline.
